title: JointLimits Object
description: Used to define limits for the range of motion of a joint.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: isMaximumValueEnabled
properties.description: Gets and sets whether the maximum joint limit is enabled or not.

properties.name: isMinimumValueEnabled
properties.description: Gets and sets whether the minimum joint limit is enabled or not.

properties.name: isRestValueEnabled
properties.description: Gets and sets whether the resting joint value is enabled or not.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: maximumValue
properties.description: The maximum value of the value. This is in either centimeters or radians depending on if the joint value this is associated with defines a distance or an angle.

properties.name: minimumValue
properties.description: The minimum value of the value. This is in either centimeters or radians depending on if the joint value this is associated with defines a distance or an angle.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: restValue
properties.description: The resting state value. This is in either centimeters or radians depending on if the joint value this is associated with defines a distance or an angle.

classType.title: JointLimits.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

isMaximumValueEnabled.title: JointLimits.isMaximumValueEnabled Property
isMaximumValueEnabled.description: Gets and sets whether the maximum joint limit is enabled or not.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = jointLimits_var.isMaximumValueEnabled

# Set the value of the property.
jointLimits_var.isMaximumValueEnabled = propertyValue
